─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Testing OpenCode Plugins ]─ ┐

Testing OpenCode Plugins

Verify that Lore’s OpenCode plugins are loading and firing correctly.

Prerequisites

  • OpenCode installed (curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/opencode-ai/opencode/refs/heads/main/install | bash)
  • A Lore project with .opencode/plugins/ and opencode.json

Quick Smoke Test

cd <lore-project>
opencode --log-level DEBUG

Send any message. Watch for log entries from these services:

  • session-init — should fire on session start, inject full banner
  • knowledge-tracker — should fire after each tool use
  • protect-memory — should fire before tool use (silent unless MEMORY.md is targeted)

Expected OpenCode session-init behavior:

  • Startup (first experimental.chat.system.transform call): inject full === LORE ... banner
  • Compaction (experimental.session.compacting): re-inject full banner
  • Per-turn (experimental.chat.system.transform): inject compact one-line reminder only

Log Locations

System logs (where client.app.log() output goes):

  • macOS/Linux: ~/.local/share/opencode/log/
  • Timestamped files, last 10 kept

Plugin session logs (if plugins write their own):

  • .opencode/logs/<session-id>.log (JSON lines)
# List session logs
ls -la .opencode/logs/

# View formatted
cat .opencode/logs/<session-id>.log | jq .

# Find all triggered actions
grep '"triggered":true' .opencode/logs/*.log | jq .

Debug Flags

# Verbose logging — shows plugin activity
opencode --log-level DEBUG

# Print logs directly to terminal
opencode --print-logs

Verifying Each Plugin

session-init

  1. Start OpenCode: opencode --log-level DEBUG
  2. Check logs for session-init service entries
  3. Confirm startup injection includes the full banner (=== LORE ... KNOWLEDGE MAP)
  4. Confirm per-turn payload is the compact one-line reminder (e.g. [Orchestrate, don't execute ... | Rules: ... | New context? → docs/knowledge/ ...])
  5. Confirm per-turn payload does not include the full banner

knowledge-tracker

  1. Ask the agent to run a bash command
  2. Check logs for knowledge-tracker service entry
  3. Message should be: “Snag (gotcha, quirk)? → fieldnote | New context? → docs/knowledge/”
  4. Run 3+ bash commands in a row — message should escalate

protect-memory

  1. Ask the agent to write to MEMORY.md
  2. The write should be blocked with routing guidance
  3. Writing to MEMORY.local.md should be allowed

Troubleshooting

Plugins not loading:

  • Check directory name: plugins/ (plural) vs plugin/ (singular) — verify against current OpenCode docs
  • Check file extension: .js vs .ts — OpenCode may expect TypeScript
  • Disable all plugins: remove plugin key from ~/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc
  • Re-enable one at a time to isolate
  • Syntax errors silently prevent loading

No log output:

  • Confirm opencode.json exists in project root with {"instructions": ["CLAUDE.md", "MEMORY.local.md"]}
  • Confirm .opencode/package.json exists with "type": "module"
  • Try --print-logs flag for terminal output

Plugin loaded but hooks not firing:

  • Hook names are case-sensitive: session.created, experimental.session.compacting, experimental.chat.system.transform, tool.execute.after, tool.execute.before
  • Check OpenCode version — experimental hook names can change between releases
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
